title: cuphea racemosa subsp. hirticaulis
intro: Cuphea racemosa subsp. hirticaulis, also known as Mexican Heather or Mexican Copperplant, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Cuphea racemosa subsp. hirticaulis are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
